explanation: An xref is a form to express the relationship between 2 entities as tuples that consist of at least the 2 representatives of these entities each. Further fields that describe the relation are allowed.Instances without a mutual relation are not represented by a tupple, whereas instances that are related are represented by exacty one tuple – so there is a uniqueness condiion for each pair of entities.This form is relational and sparse. It is not optimised for authoring but for machine processing and structured storage of data. It can be used to generate a → crosstable.

the same as an xref with the one difference that the uniqueness is violated. There are multiple sets of the same pair of entities in the data which prevents the straight transformation into a → crosstable.We provide a standard transormation, to create an xref and an addtiional table from the data though.

A crosstable is a form to express the relation between 2 entities as a matrix between instances of the 2 entities. If a matrix cell is filled, this indicates an exiting relation – optionally the text in the cell can be interpreted as additional information describing the specific relation of the 2 instances involved. This form to express the relation resembles an excel pivot table but is different because the matrix fields do not contain aggregated numerical values but one specific value. It is an authoring projection.
